CheckTitle: Review named locations configured as trusted for MFA bypass
Description: Identifies named locations marked as 'trusted' which can be used to bypass MFA requirements in Conditional Access policies. Each trusted location should be reviewed to ensure it represents a genuinely controlled network.
Risk: Named locations marked as trusted can be used in CA policy conditions to skip MFA. If an attacker can route traffic through a trusted IP range (VPN compromise, BGP hijack), they bypass MFA entirely. Over-trusting locations expands the attack surface.

Remediation.Recommendation: Review all trusted named locations and remove the trusted flag from any that do not represent corporate-controlled networks. Prefer compliant device checks over trusted location-based MFA bypass.

SourceNotes: AI-generated framework mappings — verify before citing in compliance reports.
